name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (4): 150 Years of Vande Mataram

sourceRefs.label: pmindia.gov.in/britannica.com/vandemataram150.in - 150th anniversary commemoration of Vande Mataram (2025-26); Republic Day 2026 theme; song's National Movement history

researchNotes: Fourth History/National-Movement current-affairs topic (4th lap). A strong fit for this unit specifically — the dated 2025-26 commemoration anchors a deep dive into the song's actual National Movement history (Swadeshi movement, 1905 anti-partition protests), tying current affairs directly back to core syllabus content rather than being a standalone disconnected fact.

reading.intro: Covers the year-long 150th-anniversary commemoration of 'Vande Mataram' (2025-26) and the song's real history within the Indian National Movement.

qa.question: What nationwide commemoration began on 7 November 2025, and how long does it run?
qa.answer: A year-long commemoration of 150 years of 'Vande Mataram', running from 7 November 2025 to 7 November 2026 — approved by the Union Cabinet to connect citizens, especially youth and students, with the song's revolutionary spirit.

qa.question: What was the theme of Republic Day 2026, and which anniversary did it mark?
qa.answer: Republic Day 2026's theme was '150 Years of Vande Mataram' — marking the 77th anniversary of India's Constitution.

qa.question: Who composed 'Vande Mataram', when, and in which work did it first appear in print?
qa.answer: Bankim Chandra Chatterjee composed it in 1876 (in Bengali and Sanskrit); it was featured in his 1882 novel 'Anandamath', which is how it became widely known.

qa.question: When and by whom was 'Vande Mataram' first sung at an Indian National Congress session?
qa.answer: 28 September 1896, at the INC session in Calcutta, sung by Rabindranath Tagore.

qa.question: When did 'Vande Mataram' first become a political slogan, and in connection with which movement?
qa.answer: 7 August 1905, during student processions at Calcutta Town Hall — becoming the rallying cry of the Swadeshi movement and the protest against the Partition of Bengal.

qa.question: What is 'Vande Mataram's official constitutional status in India today?
qa.answer: It is India's National SONG (not the National Anthem — that is 'Jana Gana Mana') — given equal status with the National Anthem when India became a Republic in 1950.

questions.prompt: The 150th-anniversary commemoration of 'Vande Mataram' runs from 7 November 2025 to:
'Vande Mataram' was composed by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee in 1876, and later featured in his novel:
'Vande Mataram' was first sung at an Indian National Congress session (Calcutta, 28 September 1896) by:
'Vande Mataram' became a political slogan/rallying cry of which movement, starting 7 August 1905?
questions.explanation: Swadeshi Movement, protesting the Partition of Bengal.

questions.prompt: 'Vande Mataram' holds what official status in India, distinct from the National Anthem?
options: It is the National Anthem itself
It is only a state song of West Bengal
questions.explanation: The National Song (Jana Gana Mana is the National Anthem).
